references.title: Prerequisites-based course recommendation: recommending learning objects using concept prerequisites and metadata matching

extracted.Data sources: Course metadata, Concept Corpus and Pre-Requisite Relationships,
extracted.Preprocessing Details: The first part of the work is the association between course concepts and concept pre-requisites.
extracted.Comments: Authors present an improvement to traditional educational recommender systems by proposing contents meant to address the course pre-requisites for learrners.

annotations.text: Educational recommender systems have problems with cold-start but also require learners to have pre-requisite knowledge to fully comprehend the received recommendation. The approach starts with creating pairs between concepts and pre-requisites and making this form of labelling using ML.
The similarity between pre-requisites and course metadata are the mechanism used to determine the recommendations. Results of experimental tests reveal promising Accuracy scores.
